title: Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for privacy, consent, safety, medical care options, and no pressure to disclose details. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
content: Use this entry to organize privacy, consent, safety, medical care options, and no pressure to disclose details. Start with scene safety, hand hygiene, calm communication, and written timing. Keep the person comfortable, protect privacy, avoid risky experiments, and prepare a clear handover for a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, or trained first-aider. Watch especially for: immediate danger, injury, bleeding, unconsciousness, drugging concern, pregnancy concern, or child victim. In a long emergency, review the person regularly instead of assuming they are stable.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”.
soap or sanitizer if available — for «Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route»
gloves or clean barrier if available — for «Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route»
paper and pen. Context: “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”.
phone or radio if available — for «Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route»
labels for times and medicines — for «Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route»
steps: Check that the place is safe for the helper and the injured or sick person. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.
Call emergency help or send a reliable person for help when any red flag is present. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.
Write the time the problem started, what changed, medicines taken, allergies, and known conditions. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.

Use clean hands and clean barriers before touching wounds, body fluids, food, medicines, infants, or medical devices. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.
Keep the person warm or cool as appropriate, still if trauma is possible, and protected from crowds. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.
Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.

Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”.
Escalate urgently if you see: immediate danger, injury, bleeding, unconsciousness, drugging concern, pregnancy concern, or child victim.

Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”.
common_mistakes: Waiting too long after red flags appear while carrying out “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
alternatives: Use official first-aid training when available as a fallback for “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Use local emergency numbers and pharmacists for medicine questions as a fallback for “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Use a trusted translator if language blocks care as a fallback for “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
failure_signs: Red flags appear: immediate danger, injury, bleeding, unconsciousness, drugging concern, pregnancy concern, or child victim
The person becomes harder to wake, breathe, drink, speak, walk, or recognize during “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
Pain, bleeding, swelling, fever, confusion, or weakness gets worse during “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route”
when_not_to_use: Do not delay emergency care to complete the checklist when using the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” record
Do not move a trauma patient unless staying is more dangerous when using the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” record
Do not force care on a conscious person except immediate life safety situations under local rules when using the “Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route” record
short_term: Stabilize the scene, reduce immediate harm, record key facts, and connect the person to trained help. This note applies specifically to Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route.
long_term: Keep medical records updated, train the group in certified first aid, maintain supplies, and practice respectful handovers. This note applies specifically to Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route.
if_method_fails: Escalate to emergency services, nearest clinic, pharmacist, radio medical consult, or evacuation route; document the failed step and time. This note applies specifically to Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route.
environment_notes: In apartments, heat, smoke, poor ventilation, crowds, no elevator, and water outages can make medical problems worse. Plan quiet space, clean water, light, and transport. This note applies specifically to Suspected sexual assault medical privacy route.
